page: UpCloud 32xCPU-128GB is a general-purpose server instance configured with 32 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, running on the KVM hypervisor. The processing power is delivered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. This configuration features 32 physical cores with a single thread per core and a shared CPU allocation. The memory configuration provides 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with 2048 GB of local SSD capacity, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals distinct performance characteristics. Single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring in the bottom 10% at 785.68. However, multi-core and parallelized capabilities are significantly stronger. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches 228.53 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25%. Similarly, PassMark CPU tests for extended instructions, encryption, and physics all rank in the top 25%. Other metrics, including Geekbench multi-core scores, Redis SET operations at 41.91 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 14.57 GB/sec, fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation and 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offer a dense resource profile for workloads that can leverage parallel execution without requiring dedicated, high-frequency single-core performance. The combination of 128.0 GB of memory and 2048 GB of local SSD storage makes this instance appropriate for multi-threaded data processing, database operations, static web serving, and moderate multi-core compilation tasks.
description: UpCloud 32xCPU-128GB is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 32 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running on the KVM hypervisor. The system utilizes a shared CPU allocation with 32 physical cores operating at 2.0 GHz, paired with 2048 GB of local SSD storage. While single-core CPU benchmarks place this instance in the bottom 10%, its multi-threaded capabilities are robust, with bzip3 decompression, PassMark encryption, and physics tests scoring in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and web serving throughput remain in the average tier. This hardware profile is suited for highly parallelized workloads, database operations, and multi-threaded data processing that benefit from a balanced memory-to-core ratio and substantial local storage without requiring dedicated single-core execution.
og_description: UpCloud 32xCPU-128GB is a general-purpose KVM server featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2048 GB SSD storage, offering strong multi-threaded performance despite lower single-core speeds.
meta_description: UpCloud 32xCPU-128GB is a general-purpose server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2048 GB SSD storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A multi-core virtual server featuring balanced memory, substantial local SSD storage, and strong parallel processing capabilities for distributed workloads.
bullet_points: 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128.0 GB RAM on KVM hypervisor
Strong multi-threaded performance in decompression and encryption benchmarks
Shared CPU allocation with 4.0 GB RAM per core
Appropriate for database operations, web serving, and parallel data processing
